IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
This work is taking place in The Ling area.
We are removing overhead electricity cables and replacing them with new underground electricity
cables. We will also be installing a new piece of equipment called a substation. Doing this will
improve the reliability of the electricity network and reduce the risk of you having an unplanned
power cut in the future.
To connect the new cables and complete the work safely we need to turn your power off. To
ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our contractors may carry out preparation
works prior to the power cut.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: This work is taking place on Chelmsford Road. To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified 13 poles that need upgrading and refurbishing 4 additional poles. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future, as we appreciate that you may have been affected by an unplanned power in the last 6 months. Please can customers who have solar panels isolate these to try avoid potential generator issues. During this time, you will be connected to a generator to provide you with electricity whilst we carry out the works. To connect and disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: ** These works were originally planned for the 26th of February 2026 but were cancelled **  We regularly inspect our electrical equipment to ensure it is safe and efficient. We are removing a piece of equipment from our electricity network called a pole mounted transformer and replacing the transformer with a new underground transformer, Once this is completed this will enable some overhead electricity cables to be removed. This essential work helps to provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, reducing the risk of any unplanned power cuts in the future. To keep everyone safe while we carry out these works, we will need to temporarily turn off your electricity. We are taking extra measures to limit the time your power is off and we'll do our best to cause as little disruption as possible. Doing this will improve the reliability of the electricity network in the future. This work is taking place on Newborough Road.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Lee at 08:28 - Our engineers have now restored power to all 15 customers in the Cooper Street, Ash, CT3 2 area, by diverting power around an overhead cable fault. We know a power cut can be difficult; we're sorry for any disruption this unplanned power cut caused.  

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations.

To talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Lee at 08:23 - We're sorry for the disruption to your power today, especially for those who may be more vulnerable without the electric. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. For more information just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR If you have any further concerns or questions though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hello, Lee from the messaging team here. Thanks for your patience today, our field staff and control room engineers are working to get your power back on as quickly as they can.

Our records show that some properties affected by today's power cut may have had other unexpected interruptions recently. If you have, we're sorry for further disruption. All power cuts are fully investigated by our engineers but can sometimes happen multiple times before we can safely find the issue and fix it.

We know that not everyone affected by today's power cut will have had other power cuts recently, but we want you to know that if you have, our customer services team will try to update as many customers as possible about our investigations.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.
